policy_sections.title: Boarding Requirements
policy_sections.content:
Boarding Requirements

To ensure the safety and comfort of all guests, all dogs staying with Happy Paws must meet the following general boarding requirements:

  • Dogs must be at least 4 months of age and have completed their initial puppy vaccination series.
  • All dogs over 7 months of age must be spayed or neutered to participate in group play. Intact dogs may be limited to individual play and potty breaks.
  • Dogs must be generally healthy, free from contagious illnesses, and not have vomited or experienced diarrhea within 24 hours of check-in (unless cleared by a veterinarian).
  • All dogs must be current on required vaccinations as outlined in our Health & Vaccination Policies.
  • For the safety of staff and other guests, we cannot accept dogs with a history of severe aggression toward people or other dogs.

Owners must complete a boarding profile and sign our Terms of Service prior to their dog's first stay. Please arrive 10–15 minutes early for the initial check-in to allow time for a brief health and behavior review.

policy_sections.title: Health & Vaccination Policies
policy_sections.content:
Health & Vaccination Policies

All dogs visiting Happy Paws for boarding, daycare, or grooming must be up to date on core vaccinations and be free of external parasites (fleas and ticks). Proof of vaccination from a licensed veterinarian is required before services can be provided.

Required Core Vaccinations

  • Rabies – 1-year or 3-year vaccine accepted, according to your veterinarian's protocol.
  • Distemper/Parvo (DHPP) – Must be current according to your veterinarian's recommended schedule.
  • Bordetella (Kennel Cough) – Intranasal, oral, or injectable forms accepted.

For boarding stays longer than 3 nights, dogs must have up-to-date Rabies, Distemper/Parvo (DHPP), and Bordetella vaccinations administered by a licensed veterinarian within the past 12 months (Bordetella within the past 6 months). Written proof (veterinary receipt, printout, or official app record) is required at or before check-in for extended stays.

Health & Illness

Dogs showing signs of contagious illness (such as persistent coughing, vomiting, diarrhea, nasal discharge, or unexplained lethargy) may be declined at check-in or separated from group activities for their safety and the safety of others. If your dog develops symptoms during their stay, we will contact you and your veterinarian or our on-call partner clinic as needed.

Please notify us of any chronic health conditions, recent surgeries, or special medication needs prior to arrival so we can determine if additional care charges or restrictions apply.

policy_sections.title: Terms of Service
policy_sections.content:
Terms of Service

By using Happy Paws services, you agree to our Terms of Service, including payment policies, cancellation and no-show policies, and liability limitations related to the inherent risks of animal care and group play. Owners are responsible for providing accurate information about their pet's health and behavior and for maintaining current contact information and authorized pick-up persons.

Reservations are not guaranteed until confirmed by email and secured with a valid payment method. Additional charges may apply for late pick-ups, holiday stays, medications, or special handling. Full Terms of Service are available at check-in and may be updated periodically.

policy_sections.title: Privacy Policy
policy_sections.content:
Privacy Policy

Happy Paws respects your privacy. We collect only the personal information necessary to provide services, process payments, and communicate about your reservations. This may include your name, contact details, pet information, and transaction history.

We do not sell your personal information. Limited data may be shared with third parties that help us operate our business (such as payment processors and scheduling tools), under strict confidentiality obligations. You may request access to or deletion of your personal data as permitted by law.

promotions.name: First-Time Boarder: 15% Off 3+ Nights
promotions.description: New boarding customers receive 15% off their first boarding stay of 3 or more consecutive nights. Discount applies to nightly room rates only and cannot be combined with other boarding promotions.

promotions.name: 20% Off 10-Day Daycare Passes
promotions.description: Save 20% on any 10-day daycare pass when purchased online. Valid for full-day and half-day 10-day passes.

promotions.name: Weekend Suite Upgrade - $25 Off/Night
promotions.description: Receive $25 off per night on standard and luxury suites for Friday–Sunday stays. Discount applies automatically when promo code is entered at checkout.

promotions.name: Refer-a-Friend Boarding Credit
promotions.description: Get a $25 credit toward boarding or daycare when a new customer completes their first paid stay using your referral code.

promotions.name: Welcome Back: 10% Off Boarding (Expired)
promotions.description: Returning customers previously received 10% off a single boarding stay. This promotion has ended and is no longer valid.

vaccination_requirements.description: For all boarding stays of 1 night or more, dogs must be current on core vaccinations and free of contagious illness. Required vaccinations include Rabies, Distemper/Parvo (DHPP), and Bordetella (kennel cough), administered by a licensed veterinarian according to their recommended schedule. Proof of vaccination must be submitted before or at check-in in the form of a veterinary record, receipt, or official app printout.

For boarding stays longer than 3 nights, dogs must have up-to-date Rabies, Distemper/Parvo (DHPP), and Bordetella vaccinations administered by a licensed veterinarian within the past 12 months, with Bordetella given within the last 6 months. Written proof of these vaccinations (such as a veterinary receipt, medical printout, or official app record) is required at or before check-in for extended boarding stays.

For all daycare visits, including single days and passes, dogs must be current on Rabies, Distemper/Parvo (DHPP), and Bordetella vaccinations. Bordetella must have been administered within the last 12 months, and more frequent boosters may be recommended for frequent daycare guests. Proof of vaccination from a licensed veterinarian is required before a dog may participate in group daycare play.
